top_categories.workout_context: 10,991ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Galibier Challenge's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
71 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Galibier Challenge's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 71 miles.
Base fitness for Galibier Challenge. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
155ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Galibier Challenge. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 10,991ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
At Galibier Challenge, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Below LT1, you burn fat. Above it, you burn glycogen. For 71 miles, a higher LT1 means slower bonk.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Galibier Challenge’s 71 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Galibier Challenge with 10,991ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 71 miles of mixed terrain in Saint-Michel-de-Maurienne, Savoie, France.
